meta.title: Premium Typography Rules for SOTY Event Horizon
meta.author: Cleanlystudio
meta.description: Comprehensive typography database for auditing and upgrading the Event Horizon WebGL experience

core_rules: Pair fonts with contrasting classifications but similar proportions (x-height, cap height)
Never pair two fonts from the same classification (two geometrics, two humanists)
Limit to 2-3 fonts maximum per project — one heading, one body, optional mono
Ensure sufficient visual contrast between heading and body fonts
Test pairings at multiple sizes — what works at 48px may not work at 14px

serif_plus_sans.description: Classic contrast pairing — traditional meets modern
serif_plus_sans.effect: Elegant hierarchy with clear role separation

geometric_plus_humanist.effect: Modern and approachable — tech that feels human

sans_plus_mono.description: Clean hierarchy with technical precision accent

space_grotesk.name: Space Grotesk

characteristics.style: Geometric, slightly quirky, techy yet approachable
characteristics.x_height: Tall — improves readability at smaller sizes
characteristics.contrast: Low stroke contrast — even weight distribution
characteristics.distinctive_letters: Distinctive g, a, and t — prevents generic feel

strengths: Excellent for headings and display text
Geometric precision aligns with sci-fi/tech aesthetic
Distinctive enough to be memorable, neutral enough to not distract
Good at large sizes — clean and impactful
weaknesses: Can feel too casual at very small sizes for serious data
Limited weight range compared to superfamilies
Not ideal for long-form body text (geometric sans tend to tire the eye)

headings.text_transform: uppercase for labels, sentence case for titles

body_alternative.note: Only for short paragraphs — not long-form

jetbrains_mono.name: JetBrains Mono

characteristics.x_height: Increased x-height compared to most monospace — better readability
distinctive_features: Increased height for better readability of code
Designed for reading speed, not just aesthetics

strengths: Excellent character distinction (l vs 1, O vs 0)
Wide weight range for hierarchy within mono
Ligatures add visual polish to code/data display
weaknesses: Monospaced fonts are inherently wider — use more horizontal space
Can look too 'developer tool' if overused — needs careful application
Not suitable for general body text

type_scale_ratios.description: Mathematical ratios for establishing harmonious type hierarchies

minor_second.name: Minor Second
minor_second.character: Very tight — minimal contrast between sizes. Useful for dense data interfaces.
minor_second.best_for: Dense data dashboards, small viewport experiences

minor_third.name: Minor Third
minor_third.character: Comfortable range — good for most web applications.
minor_third.best_for: General purpose web design, balanced hierarchy

major_third.name: Major Third
major_third.character: Clear hierarchy — classic web design scale.
major_third.best_for: Marketing pages, portfolios, clear visual hierarchy

perfect_fourth.name: Perfect Fourth
perfect_fourth.character: Strong hierarchy — dramatic size differences. Great for immersive experiences.

augmented_fourth.name: Augmented Fourth (√2)
augmented_fourth.character: Based on √2 — mathematically significant. Creates tension and drama.
augmented_fourth.best_for: Dramatic presentations, experimental design, Event Horizon chapter titles

perfect_fifth.name: Perfect Fifth
perfect_fifth.music_analog: Perfect fifth — the most consonant interval
perfect_fifth.character: Very dramatic — large jumps between sizes. Bold and impactful.
perfect_fifth.best_for: Poster design, hero sections, cinematic typography

golden_ratio.name: Golden Ratio (φ)
golden_ratio.character: The most aesthetically pleasing ratio found in nature. Extreme hierarchy.
golden_ratio.best_for: Art-directed one-off pages, maximum visual drama

recommended_for_event_horizon.reasoning: The √2 ratio creates dramatic size differences that match the cosmic scale of the experience. The 'tritone' musical analog (historically called 'diabolus in musica') fits the ominous black hole theme.

notes: 1.4 for tight, modern designs with short paragraphs
1.6 for long-form content or wider columns
On dark backgrounds, increase by 0.05-0.1 (text feels tighter on dark)
1.2-1.3 for smaller headings (h3-h6) that approach body size
Very large text (64px+) needs tighter line height
0.9 can work for uppercase display text
1.0 is safe for most display scenarios
Negative line height (< 1.0) creates visual density and weight
Monospaced text needs more vertical space due to uniform width

size_dependent_adjustment.reason: Small text needs more leading for readability

general_principles: Tracking (letter-spacing) should DECREASE as font size INCREASES
Uppercase text needs MORE tracking than mixed case
Light font weights need slightly MORE tracking than bold
Monospaced fonts should never have letter-spacing modified (defeats purpose)
Geometric sans-serifs tolerate tighter tracking than humanist

uppercase_labels.note: Essential for readability of uppercase text. Without tracking, uppercase feels cramped.

body_text.note: Body text usually needs no adjustment — the font designer optimized for this.

subheadings_24_36.note: Slightly tighter than body for visual weight.

display_72_plus.note: Large text looks too loose at default tracking.

small_caps.note: Small caps need extra tracking for readability.

dark_background_adjustment.description: Text on dark backgrounds suffers from halation (light spread). Compensate with slightly wider tracking.
dark_background_adjustment.adjustment: +0.01em to +0.02em compared to light background values
dark_background_adjustment.applies_to: Body text and small text. Headings are less affected.

rules: 45-75 characters per line is the established optimal range (Robert Bringhurst, Elements of Typographic Style)
65 characters is the ideal — most comfortable for sustained reading
Below 40 characters: too many line breaks, feels choppy
Above 80 characters: eye loses its place when returning to next line
For immersive experiences with short text blocks, 40-55 characters works well (more intimate)
Monospaced text: reduce by 10-15 characters (80ch mono ≈ 65ch proportional)

implementation.css_ch_unit: max-width: 65ch — uses the '0' character width as reference
implementation.centered_text: Text blocks should be centered with max-width for long-form in immersive experiences

event_horizon_specific.narrative_text: max-width: 55ch — intimate, poetic feel for story text
event_horizon_specific.data_readouts: No max-width restriction — data fills available space
event_horizon_specific.chapter_titles: No max-width — display text sizes self-limit naturally

character_by_character.best_for: Chapter titles, key phrases, impactful moments
character_by_character.performance: Split text with GSAP SplitText or manual span wrapping. GPU-composited transforms only.
character_by_character.gsap_example: gsap.from('.char', { opacity: 0, y: 20, stagger: 0.03, duration: 0.6, ease: 'back.out(1.7)' })

word_by_word.performance: Lighter than character animation — fewer elements to animate

line_by_line.best_for: Body text paragraphs, lists, longer content
line_by_line.performance: Most performant text animation — fewest animated elements

typewriter.description: Characters appear as if being typed

blur_reveal.description: Text starts blurred and comes into focus
blur_reveal.performance_warning: filter: blur is expensive — use sparingly and only on GPU-composited layers

mask_reveal.description: Text revealed by a moving mask/clip-path
mask_reveal.best_for: Elegant reveals, editorial style, premium feel
mask_reveal.performance: Excellent — clipPath is GPU-composited in modern browsers

scramble_resolve.description: Characters cycle through random glyphs before resolving to correct text
scramble_resolve.best_for: Sci-fi aesthetic, data decryption, glitch effects
scramble_resolve.event_horizon_use: Scientific data resolving, chapter number reveals

gravity_pull.description: Characters fall or are pulled toward a central point (singularity)
gravity_pull.physics: Characters accelerate toward black hole center, spaghettify
gravity_pull.best_for: Event Horizon specific — text being consumed by the void
gravity_pull.implementation: Custom GSAP timeline with per-character physics calculation

scroll_driven.note: Duration becomes progress-based — 0 to 1 mapped to scroll position

easing_for_text.appear: power2.out or power3.out — decelerate into resting position
easing_for_text.float: sine.inOut — gentle oscillation for ambient text

font_weight_adjustment.description: Light text on dark backgrounds appears heavier due to halation (light bleed). Compensate by using lighter weights.
rules: If you'd use 400 (regular) on light, use 300 (light) on dark
If you'd use 700 (bold) on light, use 600 (semibold) on dark — or keep 700 but reduce size
Very thin weights (100-200) work better on dark backgrounds for large display text
The effect is more pronounced on lower-resolution screens and non-Retina displays

antialiasing.windows: No equivalent — Windows ClearType handles this differently
antialiasing.note: These properties thin the rendered font — perfect for dark backgrounds, too thin for light backgrounds

text_shadow_for_legibility.over_moving_content: Add a subtle dark vignette or backdrop behind text areas
text_shadow_for_legibility.principle: On WebGL backgrounds, text needs either shadow, backdrop, or guaranteed contrast zone

backdrop_techniques.name: Gradient fade
backdrop_techniques.use: Text at bottom of full-screen visuals

backdrop_techniques.name: Frosted glass
backdrop_techniques.use: Floating text panels over dynamic content

backdrop_techniques.name: Safe zone
backdrop_techniques.description: Render a guaranteed dark area in the WebGL scene behind text regions

fluid_type.description: Type size scales smoothly between viewport breakpoints using CSS clamp()
fluid_type.technique: preferred value uses viewport units for smooth scaling

examples.explanation: Scales from 14px on small screens to 18px on large screens

event_horizon_specific.canvas_overlay_text: Use absolute positioning with viewport units for text overlaying the WebGL canvas
event_horizon_specific.dynamic_sizing: Chapter title size should correlate with viewport height (vh units) for cinematic impact
event_horizon_specific.mobile_narrative: On mobile (< 768px), narrative text needs larger relative size (18-20px) due to closer reading distance

sdf_rendering.name: Signed Distance Field text rendering
sdf_rendering.description: Pre-compute distance field for each glyph — allows crisp text at any scale with a single texture
advantages: Resolution-independent — scales cleanly from tiny to huge
Efficient — one texture atlas serves all sizes
Supports effects: outlines, shadows, glow — all in shader

libraries.name: troika-three-text
libraries.description: Best SDF text for Three.js — handles layout, wrapping, styling

libraries.name: three-bmfont-text
libraries.description: Bitmap font rendering — fast but limited styling

performance_tips: Generate SDF atlas at build time, not runtime
Use MSDF (multi-channel) for complex glyphs and sharp corners
Batch text meshes that share the same font/material
Disable raycasting on text meshes if not interactive

pros: Easy to style and animate with CSS/GSAP
cons: Cannot participate in 3D scene (no depth testing)
html_overlay.best_for: HUD elements, narrative text, UI overlays

webgl_text.best_for: In-scene labels, 3D floating text, text that interacts with black hole

html_overlay_vs_webgl_text.recommendation: Use HTML overlay for 90% of text (narrative, HUD, UI). Use WebGL text only for text that must exist IN the 3D scene (being pulled into black hole, floating in space).

challenges: Constantly changing luminance behind text makes contrast ratio dynamic
Motion behind text competes for attention and reduces reading comprehension
Particle effects can overlap with text strokes

solutions.name: Dedicated text safe zone
solutions.description: Reserve screen regions where the WebGL scene is guaranteed dark/consistent
solutions.implementation: Shader uniform or render mask that dims/clears areas behind text

solutions.name: Text backdrop layer
solutions.description: CSS semi-transparent dark panel behind text
solutions.implementation: position: absolute; background: rgba(0,0,0,0.5); backdrop-filter: blur(8px)

solutions.name: Dynamic contrast
solutions.description: Sample the WebGL framebuffer behind text and adjust text color/shadow accordingly
solutions.implementation: Read pixels from render target, compute average luminance, toggle text color

solutions.name: Temporal separation
solutions.description: Dim or pause background animation when text appears
solutions.implementation: Fade background to 30% opacity during text reveal, restore after reading time

awwwards_typography_examples.technique: 3D text integrated into WebGL scene with physics
awwwards_typography_examples.takeaway: Text as a 3D object — playful and memorable

awwwards_typography_examples.technique: Oversized serif headings with smooth scroll reveal
awwwards_typography_examples.takeaway: Typography as the primary visual element — the font IS the design

awwwards_typography_examples.technique: Minimal type over immersive WebGL backgrounds
awwwards_typography_examples.takeaway: Restraint in typography lets the experience breathe

awwwards_typography_examples.technique: Playful type animations with physics and interaction
awwwards_typography_examples.takeaway: Typography can be interactive and experiential

awwwards_typography_examples.technique: Clean geometric sans with extreme whitespace
awwwards_typography_examples.takeaway: Whitespace and restraint signal premium quality
